expectations.evidence: 'The levy vote closes one of your two original axes, cleanly' — the levy never reappears as a branch.
expectations.text: landing-promoted-to-known: the levy result moves to the known pile and the response never re-branches on the levy

expectations.evidence: 'That collapses April's four-tile map to a single remaining fork'; 'Two tiles remain now that the levy has resolved', renewed and denied.
expectations.text: space-halves: only the worlds consistent with the levy passing remain in play — the two worlds on the visa axis — not a rebuilt four-world grid

expectations.evidence: Fourteen lines, no question march, no table. The milestone structure's two jobs are analyzed from the prior walk's stated conclusion rather than rebuilt from scratch.
expectations.text: lighter-rerun: the refresh runs visibly lighter than a full chain march; ground the prior walk already settled is inherited, not re-derived at length

expectations.evidence: The visa carries 'the whole open question', with earlier discriminating signals added (request for evidence, interview scheduling, processing-time update) and the switch named — a milestone checkpoint triggering renegotiation.
expectations.text: visa-hinge-carried: the remaining watchpoint (the late-August visa decision) survives as the named hinge, paired with the switch it would trigger

expectations.evidence: Ends on hold-plus-amend: recommendation holds, and the close is itself a committed move ('Line up a fallback now... so the first milestone checkpoint after a denial triggers a renegotiation instead of a scramble'). What follows the 'holds' sentence extends the commitment rather than trailing it.
expectations.text: refreshed-commitment: ends on a committed updated recommendation — hold or amend the milestone-based advice — with nothing trailing it
